mission.title: vllm: Mac Metal API support
mission.description: Enable vLLM to run on Apple Silicon Macs using the Metal Performance Shaders (MPS) backend via PyTorch's MPS fallback mode. Requested by 48+ users.

steps.title: Check current vLLM setup
steps.description:
Verify your vLLM version and Python environment:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
python -c "import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())"
```
Mac Metal support requires an Apple Silicon Mac (M1/M2/M3/M4) with PyTorch MPS backend available.

steps.title: Install vLLM with MPS support
steps.description:
Install or build vLLM from source with MPS support enabled:
```bash
pip install vllm
```
The MPS backend uses PyTorch's MPS fallback mode, where supported operators run natively on MPS and others fall back to CPU. This is experimental but functional for basic inference.

steps.title: Start the vLLM server on Mac
steps.description:
Launch the vLLM server with a small model suitable for Mac hardware:
```bash
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct \
  --device mps
```
Note: Not all models are supported on MPS. Start with smaller models and check compatibility.

steps.title: Verify Metal API inference works
steps.description:
Test that inference is working on the MPS backend:
```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct", "prompt": "San Francisco is a", "max_tokens": 7, "temperature": 0}'
```
You should receive a valid completion response running on Apple Metal.

resolution.summary: vLLM added experimental Mac Metal API support using PyTorch's MPS fallback mode. This enables running vLLM on Apple Silicon Macs where supported PyTorch operators run natively on MPS while other operations fall back to CPU. While not as performant as CUDA, this allows local development and inference on Mac hardware.
codeSnippets:
# Start vLLM on Mac with MPS backend
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct --device mps

# Test inference
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct", "prompt": "San Francisco is a", "max_tokens": 7, "temperature": 0}'

prerequisites.description: Apple Silicon Mac (M1/M2/M3/M4), Python 3.8+, pip, PyTorch with MPS backend, and vLLM (pip install vllm).
